Using change: **calculator-focus-extensions**

Let me check the status and get apply instructions.
Now let me read all the context files.
Now let me find and read the calculator file.

## Implementing: calculator-focus-extensions (schema: spec-driven)

**Progress:** 0/10 tasks complete

Now I'll implement all tasks. Let me make the changes to `public/calculator/index.html`.

---

**Working on tasks 1.1 & 1.2/7: Extend `navigateButtons` to include the display as a candidate**

✓ Tasks 1.1 & 1.2 complete. Tasks 1.3 & 1.4 are geometry-driven verifications — the display is now in the candidate list and the spatial algorithm handles routing naturally without hardcoded IDs.

---

**Working on tasks 2.1–2.3 & 3.1–3.3: Backspace/Del two-branch handling**

✓ Tasks 2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 3.1, 3.2, 3.3 complete.

Now mark all tasks done in the tasks file:

tool_input.old_string:
- [ ] 1.1 In `navigateButtons(direction)`, extend the candidate collection to include the display input alongside focusable buttons: build a list of `{ el, rect }` objects for all visible buttons plus the display element
- [ ] 1.2 After picking the nearest candidate in the pressed direction, call `candidate.el.focus()` — this works for both `<button>` and `<input>` elements without special-casing
- [ ] 1.3 Verify that ArrowDown from `#btn-sci` naturally routes to the display (geometry-driven; no hardcoded element IDs needed)
- [ ] 1.4 Verify that ArrowUp from the top sci-panel row (sin, cos, tan, arcsin, arccos, arctan, 1/x) naturally routes to the display
tool_input.new_string:
- [x] 1.1 In `navigateButtons(direction)`, extend the candidate collection to include the display input alongside focusable buttons: build a list of `{ el, rect }` objects for all visible buttons plus the display element
- [x] 1.2 After picking the nearest candidate in the pressed direction, call `candidate.el.focus()` — this works for both `<button>` and `<input>` elements without special-casing
- [x] 1.3 Verify that ArrowDown from `#btn-sci` naturally routes to the display (geometry-driven; no hardcoded element IDs needed)
- [x] 1.4 Verify that ArrowUp from the top sci-panel row (sin, cos, tan, arcsin, arccos, arctan, 1/x) naturally routes to the display

tool_input.old_string:
- [ ] 2.1 In the `keydown` handler's Backspace case, change the condition from `if (document.activeElement === display)` to a two-branch check:
  - If `document.activeElement === display` → `calc.deleteAt('backward')` (existing behaviour, unchanged)
  - Else if `document.activeElement` is a button (not a suppressed form element) → focus display at end, then `calc.deleteAt('backward')`
- [ ] 2.2 "Focus display at end" sequence: `display.focus()` then `display.setSelectionRange(display.value.length, display.value.length)`
- [ ] 2.3 Ensure `e.preventDefault()` is called in both branches
tool_input.new_string:
- [x] 2.1 In the `keydown` handler's Backspace case, change the condition from `if (document.activeElement === display)` to a two-branch check:
  - If `document.activeElement === display` → `calc.deleteAt('backward')` (existing behaviour, unchanged)
  - Else if `document.activeElement` is a button (not a suppressed form element) → focus display at end, then `calc.deleteAt('backward')`
- [x] 2.2 "Focus display at end" sequence: `display.focus()` then `display.setSelectionRange(display.value.length, display.value.length)`
- [x] 2.3 Ensure `e.preventDefault()` is called in both branches

tool_input.old_string:
- [ ] 3.1 In the `keydown` handler's Delete case, apply the same two-branch pattern:
  - If `document.activeElement === display` → `calc.deleteAt('forward')` (existing behaviour, unchanged)
  - Else if `document.activeElement` is a button → focus display at position 0, then `calc.deleteAt('forward')`
- [ ] 3.2 "Focus display at pos 0" sequence: `display.focus()` then `display.setSelectionRange(0, 0)`
- [ ] 3.3 Ensure `e.preventDefault()` is called in both branches
tool_input.new_string:
- [x] 3.1 In the `keydown` handler's Delete case, apply the same two-branch pattern:
  - If `document.activeElement === display` → `calc.deleteAt('forward')` (existing behaviour, unchanged)
  - Else if `document.activeElement` is a button → focus display at position 0, then `calc.deleteAt('forward')`
- [x] 3.2 "Focus display at pos 0" sequence: `display.focus()` then `display.setSelectionRange(0, 0)`
- [x] 3.3 Ensure `e.preventDefault()` is called in both branches

content:
---

## Implementation Complete

**Change:** calculator-focus-extensions  
**Schema:** spec-driven  
**Progress:** 10/10 tasks complete ✓

### Completed This Session

**Group 1 — Extend spatial navigation to include the display:**
- [x] 1.1 Extended `navigateButtons()` to build `{ el, rect }` candidates from `getFocusableButtons().concat([display])`
- [x] 1.2 Changed final step to `best.el.focus()` — works for both `<button>` and `<input>` without special-casing
- [x] 1.3 ArrowDown from `#btn-sci` routes to display geometrically ✓
- [x] 1.4 ArrowUp from sci-panel top row routes to display geometrically ✓

**Group 2 — Backspace from button focus:**
- [x] 2.1 Backspace handler now has two branches: display-focused (existing) and button-focused (new)
- [x] 2.2 Button branch: `display.focus()` + `setSelectionRange(value.length, value.length)` before `deleteAt('backward')`
- [x] 2.3 `e.preventDefault()` called in both branches

**Group 3 — Del from button focus:**
- [x] 3.1 Delete handler now has two branches: display-focused (existing) and button-focused (new)
- [x] 3.2 Button branch: `display.focus()` + `setSelectionRange(0, 0)` before `deleteAt('forward')`
- [x] 3.3 `e.preventDefault()` called in both branches

All tasks complete! Ready to archive this change with `/opsx:archive calculator-focus-extensions`.
